raw_consumer_statement:
My son signed up for a free trial at XXXX but the company actually charged our some for the full year {$230.00} ( on XX/XX/XXXX ) within the 7 day trial. This companys deceptive pricing and mice-type terms and conditions are severely deceptive and we have disputed this {$230.00} charge on our Amex 3 times but each time AMEX tells us that the dispute is in process but then ends up charging us yet again for this service that we NEVER received. My son canceled the fraudulent service the same day - XX/XX/XXXX We / my son has a confirmation that the service was canceled that same day XX/XX/XXXX ). The QR code that my son obtained never worked. 

Unfortunately, this company is deceptive practices of not refunding money even though he was fraudulently charged for the full year is unbelievable. 

Its illegal to charge for services that we as Americans dont get the actual service. Amex has violated the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) We disputed this {$230.00} charge online via American Express.com and then after we received the dispute rejection letter- we called AMEX customer service to dispute this same charge again. We never received any of the QR code services from this scammy/ shady company We do not know what else to do - American Express is usually really good about having our back on deceptive charges like this, but apparently nope this time. We have been loyal customers of AMEX since XXXX.
